reason: This looks like your antivirus. Mganga will not touch the thing that protects you.
The little shield icon that watches over Windows Security. It costs almost nothing.
Part of Windows' built-in protection. Mganga will not touch it.
Google's updater, it keeps Chrome patched against security holes. It runs briefly and goes away.
Keeps Microsoft Edge patched against security holes. Browsers are worth keeping up to date.
Adobe's update checker. Acrobat also checks for updates when you actually open it.
This is a printer utility. It only needs to run when you actually print, scan, or update, not at every boot. If your printer acts up, turn it back on.
Java's update reminder. It mostly shows popups, Java keeps working without it.
Dropbox's separate update checker. Dropbox updates itself while it runs.
Syncs your OneDrive files from the moment you log in. If you do not need files synced right away, you can start it yourself when you want it.
Syncs your MEGA cloud files at every logon. Turn it off if syncing can wait until you open MEGAsync yourself.
Syncs your Dropbox from the moment you log in. Off is fine if you can wait until you open it.
Keeps Google Drive synced from logon. Your files stay safe either way, this is about when syncing starts.
Steam works fine when you open it yourself. Starting at boot only saves you one double click.
The Epic launcher does nothing useful in the background. Open it when you want to play.
Starts Discord with Windows so messages arrive right away. If you open it when you need it, this can be off.
Spotify starts quickly when you want music. It does not need to launch with Windows.
ASUS's hub for lighting and fan profiles. Your saved settings keep applying even when it is off, you mainly need it to change them.
ASUS support software. Windows runs fine without it, but things like special keyboard keys or lighting control may stop until it is back on.
Controls Corsair lighting and devices. Off is fine if you do not change those settings often.
Razer's device software. Your mouse and keyboard keep working without it, custom profiles and lighting need it.
Logitech's device software. Basic mouse and keyboard functions work without it, custom buttons need it.
This is part of how your audio works. Leaving it on is the safe choice.
Supports your NVIDIA graphics card and its control panel. Leaving it on is the safe choice.
An audio enhancement layer that ships with some PCs. Sound keeps working without it, just without the extra processing.
Keeps this PC reachable from outside at any time. If you only use TeamViewer now and then, starting it when you need it is also the safer habit.
Keeps this PC reachable from outside at any time. Start it when you actually need remote access, that is also the safer habit.
Lets you reach this PC through Chrome Remote Desktop at any time. Off means you cannot connect remotely until you turn it back on.
Starts Teams so meetings and pings arrive at logon. If you do not live in Teams, open it when needed.
Starts Slack with Windows so messages arrive right away. Off is fine if you open it yourself.
Zoom opens fast when you click a meeting link. It does not need to wait in the background from boot.
This chat app starts fine when you open it. It does not need to launch with Windows.
Internet Download Manager's helper, it waits in the background to catch downloads from your browser. Keep it if you use IDM daily, otherwise off is fine.
Lets the Figma website use fonts installed on this PC. Only needed while you design in a browser, if fonts go missing in Figma turn it back on.
Keeps your JetBrains tools updated and ready in the tray. Your IDEs run fine without it, open Toolbox when you want updates.
Your torrent app does not need to start with Windows unless you seed around the clock.
Connects your phone to this PC at logon for messages and photos. Off is fine if you rarely use Phone Link.
Runs your clipboard, including clipboard history. Windows plumbing worth leaving alone.
Delivers your app notifications. Windows plumbing worth leaving alone.
Connects your devices for features like shared clipboard and continuing tasks across PCs.
Part of Windows' protection against dangerous websites. Mganga will not touch it.
